title: ConvertKit vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool fits your needs.
h1: ConvertKit vs Campaign Monitor: Detailed Comparison
intro: ConvertKit and Campaign Monitor are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit targets creators and course sellers with creator-focused features, while Campaign Monitor focuses on agencies and teams managing multiple brands. Choosing between them depends on your business model, team size, and feature priorities.

comparison_table.tool_b: Agencies and teams managing multiple brands

comparison_table.tool_a: Visual automations, landing pages, commerce, subscriber tagging, sequences
comparison_table.tool_b: Multi-brand management, journey designer, smart segments, transactional email, template builder

choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course seller who needs landing pages and commerce features
You want a free tier to test the platform before committing financially
You prefer a tool specifically built with creator workflows in mind
choose_b_if: You're an agency or team managing multiple client brands
You need multi-brand management and transactional email capabilities
You want the lowest entry price for a paid platform

faq.q: Is ConvertKit better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit scores higher on review sites (4.4 vs 4.1 on G2) and is purpose-built for creators with features like landing pages and commerce. Campaign Monitor excels for agencies with multi-brand management. Neither is objectively 'better'—they're optimized for different audiences.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor starts at $11/month, making it cheaper upfront. However, ConvertKit offers a free tier for up to 1,000 subscribers, which is ideal if you're just starting out. Once you exceed 1,000 subscribers, ConvertKit's $29/month plan becomes the paid entry point.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support list imports, so migrating your subscribers is straightforward. ConvertKit's interface is particularly user-friendly for creators switching from other platforms. Both offer customer support to help with the migration process.
